reason: historical attempt import from vela-science/erdos-frontier@121807e20b06eb8148f594dfe13e440181252b0f:attack/attempt-ledger.v2.json (provenance, not a verdict)

attempt.claim: lcm(1..p_{k+1}-1) < p_k*lcm(1..p_k)  <=>  P(k) := prod of bases q of prime powers q^m (m>=2) in the gap (p_k,p_{k+1}) is < p_k.
depends_on: a fully unconditional proof still needs effective prime-gap-vs-prime-square bounds (BHP p^0.525 exceeds sqrt(p), so does not trivially close it)
attempt.detail: Since no prime lies in (p_k,p_{k+1}), primes <= p_{k+1}-1 equal p_1..p_k, so the lcm ratio is exactly prod over prime powers q^m (m>=2) in the gap of q. Conjecture <=> that product < p_k. Tightest case k=4: gap (7,11) has 8=2^3,9=3^2, product 6<7. A violation needs two prime squares q1^2,q2^2 in one gap with q1*q2>=p_k, forcing gap length >= ~2*sqrt(p_k).
attempt.insight: docs/erdos-attack/458/verify458.py: 0 counterexamples for all prime gaps to p_k~3.6e8 (re-checked to 5e6); exact integer arithmetic.
attempt.reusable_for: any problem reducing to 'prime powers (m>=2) inside a prime gap' or to lcm(1..n)/Chebyshev psi growth

caveats: A signed banked attempt. Trust is earned via its verifier_attachments + the gate, never from claimed_status.
